levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the functional description for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.

The module describes the top-level functions performed by the hybrid-electric architecture, the functional relationships between major layers, the principal operating states, and the controlled functional boundaries applicable to ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Functional Scope
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture provides controlled propulsion-energy management, electrical power conversion and distribution, propulsion drive coordination, thermal-limit management, system monitoring, degraded-mode response, maintenance reporting, and ground support interfacing.

This module is descriptive only. It does not provide maintenance procedures, servicing instructions, fault isolation tasks, operational checklists, or test procedures. Those subjects shall be defined in the applicable task, fault, test, and servicing data modules.

levelledPara.title: Primary Functions
levelledPara.para: The architecture performs the following primary functions:

listItem.para: Receive and validate aircraft-level propulsion-energy demand.
Manage energy source availability and conversion readiness.
Condition, route, isolate, and protect electrical power paths.
Command and supervise propulsion drive equipment.
Maintain electrical and thermal equipment within defined limits.
Monitor system health, configuration, and operating state.
Detect abnormal conditions and initiate protective responses.
Generate crew indications, maintenance messages, and diagnostic records.

levelledPara.title: Energy Management Function
levelledPara.para: The energy management function coordinates available propulsion energy with aircraft-level demand, system operating limits, equipment availability, thermal capacity, and degraded-mode constraints.

This function supports controlled allocation of energy between conversion equipment, storage or buffer elements, propulsion drive equipment, and applicable auxiliary loads. The detailed allocation logic shall be defined by the applicable control and system design data modules.

levelledPara.title: Electrical Power Function
levelledPara.para: The electrical power function conditions and distributes electrical power to the propulsion drive equipment and associated hybrid-electric loads. It provides switching, isolation, protection, current and voltage monitoring, and fault containment at the controlled architecture boundaries.

levelledPara.title: Propulsion Drive Function
levelledPara.para: The propulsion drive function converts controlled electrical power into commanded propulsive output. It coordinates electric-machine operation, drive electronics, local sensors, protection functions, and interfaces to the propulsion control system.
The function shall maintain controlled response to command inputs, equipment limits, detected faults, and system-level operating mode transitions.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Management Function
levelledPara.para: The thermal management function removes, transports, rejects, or redistributes heat generated by energy conversion equipment, electrical distribution equipment, propulsion drive equipment, and associated control electronics.
This function supports equipment protection, availability management, degraded-mode operation, and maintenance reporting when thermal limits are approached or exceeded.

levelledPara.title: Control Coordination Function
levelledPara.para: The control coordination function manages state transitions between normal, monitored, limited, degraded, and safe-state operation. It arbitrates commands, validates system readiness, supervises protection thresholds, and coordinates responses across propulsion, electrical, thermal, and maintenance reporting functions.

levelledPara.title: Monitoring and Recording Function
levelledPara.para: The monitoring and recording function collects equipment status, operating parameters, fault indications, configuration data, and diagnostic information from the hybrid-electric architecture.
The function provides data to crew indication, aircraft recording, central maintenance, diagnostic, and ground support systems as applicable to the aircraft configuration.

levelledPara.title: Protection Function
levelledPara.para: The protection function prevents uncontrolled propagation of faults across the hybrid-electric architecture. It supports detection, isolation, limitation, shutdown, safe-state transition, and maintenance reporting for abnormal electrical, thermal, propulsion-drive, or control conditions.
Protection thresholds, response times, inhibit conditions, and reset logic shall be controlled by the applicable system safety, certification, control, and maintenance data modules.

levelledPara.title: Degraded-Mode Function
levelledPara.para: The degraded-mode function maintains a controlled operating condition after detected equipment degradation, partial loss of function, or limited system availability. The function shall support continued safe operation, controlled load reduction, dispatch limitation, or safe-state transition as defined by the certified configuration.

levelledPara.title: Maintenance Reporting Function
levelledPara.para: The maintenance reporting function generates maintenance messages, fault records, built-in-test results, configuration status, and diagnostic data for use by maintenance personnel and support systems.
Fault isolation logic, maintenance message mapping, corrective action references, and return-to-service checks shall be specified in the applicable fault isolation and maintenance data modules.

levelledPara.title: Ground Support Function
levelledPara.para: The ground support function provides controlled access to diagnostic, configuration, test, and maintenance support interfaces. Ground support interactions shall preserve system safety, electrical isolation, data integrity, and configuration traceability.

levelledPara.title: Functional Boundaries
levelledPara.para: The functional boundary of this architecture is limited to the hybrid-electric propulsion-energy management, electrical distribution, propulsion drive, thermal management, monitoring, and maintenance support functions associated with ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

Aircraft-level flight control laws, cockpit procedures, detailed dispatch policy, hydrogen system detail design, and detailed certification means of compliance are outside the scope of this data module unless explicitly referenced by the approved programme baseline.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module supports traceability between top-level functional architecture, S1000D CSDB descriptive content, system interface definition, maintenance reporting logic, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, programme configuration baseline, safety assessment evidence, and verification records before CSDB release.
